product: MTD108a (L-Acoustics MTD Line; covered together with MTD112b and MTD115b)

electroacoustic.frequency_range_hz: 85-20000 Hz (+/-3 dB), LLC FRONT position; usable bandwidth 65-20000 Hz (-10 dB)
electroacoustic.max_spl_note: Per-enclosure system output at 1 m including preset EQ (freefield): 122 dB peak / 116 dB continuous in FRONT mode, 123 dB peak / 117 dB continuous in MONITOR/XOVER mode. The legacy MTD108 datasheet listed 118 dB continuous SPL at 1 m (half-space, pink noise).
electroacoustic.directivity: Axi-symmetrical conical 100 degrees (+/-15 degrees), identical horizontal and vertical coverage
electroacoustic.components: 1x 8-inch weather-resistant low-frequency loudspeaker (2-inch voice coil) coaxially loaded by 1x 1-inch exit neodymium compression driver (titanium diaphragm); passive crossover at 2 kHz
electroacoustic.sensitivity_db: 94 dB SPL (2.83 Vrms at 1 m, 85-20k Hz)
electroacoustic.power_handling: 250 Wrms long-term / 1000 W peak at 8 ohms (45 Vrms, pink noise 6 dB crest factor)

operating_modes: passive (single-channel, single amplifier channel per enclosure)
amplification.preset_note: The original MTD line was processed by the dedicated MTD108LLCa analog line-level controller (FRONT mode adds a low-frequency contour; XOVER/MONITOR mode applies a 125 Hz high-pass filter for use with a subwoofer) and powered by legacy LAa-series amplifiers (LA15a/LA17a/LA24a/LA48a). Amplifier mapping (LA4, max 8 per amp) and the single amp channel reflect the calculator's current archive-bundle configuration.
amplification.recommended_power_legacy: Manufacturer-recommended amplifier power was 500 W into 8 ohms (one enclosure); a single amp channel can drive up to four parallel MTD108a (2 ohms, 2000 W recommended).

connection.input_connector: 2x 4-pin Neutrik speakON (NL4), wired in parallel for daisy-chaining / paralleling enclosures (pins 1+/1- active, 2+/2- no connection)

physical.cabinet: 15 mm and 18 mm Baltic birch plywood, sealed, screwed and rabbeted joints; maroon-gray (RAL 8019) high-resiliency paint; front protected by 1.5 mm powder-coated steel grille over acoustically transparent open-cell foam
physical.trap_angle: 22.5 or 45 degrees with respect to vertical (for floor-monitor use)

modes: wall mount via Omnimount bracket (4 integral rear attachment points)
ceiling / wall / scaffold mount via ETR8 adjustable U-bracket (horizontal or vertical orientation)
floor monitor (22.5 degree short-throw or 45 degree long-throw listening angle)
rigging_accessories: PION1 single-stud Aeroquip flytrack fitting-to-ring (safety attachment)
deployment.rigging_note: ETR8 has a working load limit of 10.5 daN with an ultimate-strength safety factor greater than 12; mountable via 1, 2 or 3 of the 9 mm bracket holes. Manufacturer recommends dissociating (spacing) individual enclosures rather than tightly coupling them to preserve true point-source behavior and avoid comb filtering.

usage: Compact passive coaxial enclosure best suited to distributed and proximity reinforcement: delay rings, front fill, under-balcony fills, surround/effects channels, and speech reinforcement in theatres, clubs, houses of worship, retail, and AV installs.
Each enclosure is passive and single-channel, so it needs one amplifier channel; the calculator pairs it with the LA4, fitting up to 8 enclosures per amplifier when wired across its channels.
At a nominal 8 ohms, up to four MTD108a can be paralleled on one amp channel (down to 2 ohms); plan amplifier headroom around roughly 500 W per enclosure to avoid clipping the HF driver.
Daisy-chain enclosures through the two parallel speakON connectors, keeping cable runs short and gauge low-resistance to maintain damping factor.
Doubles as a high-efficiency floor monitor using the 22.5 degree (short-throw) or 45 degree (long-throw) trap angles.
Extend low end and SPL by adding an SB115/SB118/SB218 subwoofer crossed over around 100-150 Hz; the XOVER preset applies a 125 Hz high-pass for sub integration.
source_manual: MTD108a / MTD112b / MTD115b Operator Manual V1.2 (EN)
